_comment: CANONICAL Contoso M365 workbook dataset (Phase 90.2 Plan 01, Task 1). This is the SINGLE source of truth for the deterministic Customers/Orders rows, the pinned reference date + 3-month window boundary, the per-id Excel range addresses, and the hand-computed expected headline set. tests/fixtures/contoso-m365.toml (script addresses), tests/fixtures/contoso-m365-scenarios.yaml (asserted values), AND Plans 02 (wiremock rows) / 03 (Code Mode REF + expected set) TRANSCRIBE from here -- they MUST NOT invent independent values. Inert static business data; never executed. NO secrets, NO Date-derived values (every date is a hand-written literal).
_window_note: window_start = ref_date minus 3 calendar months, written as a literal. ref_date day is 28 (<= 28) so the minus-3-months boundary is calendar-unambiguous (no 31->30/28 trap). The headline query window is the inclusive interval [window_start, ref_date] = [2026-02-28, 2026-05-28]. Plan 03's `const REF` copies ref_date verbatim; Plan 03 may also assert against window_start.
_addressing_note: Customers sheet: row 1 is the header, customer data starts at row 2. customer_id 'C00N' -> index N -> sheet row = N + 1 (header offset 1). Single-customer range is `A{row}:D{row}` over columns A..D = customer_id,name,segment,region. Orders sheet: row 1 is the header, order data starts at row 2, laid out grouped/sorted by customer_id so each customer's orders form a contiguous block (D-03). columns A..D = order_id,customer_id,order_date,amount.
_orders_blocks_note: C006 has NO orders (intentional non-match: no in-window orders at all), so it has no contiguous block address here. get_customer_orders('C006') reads an empty result.
_expected_note: Hand-computed: customers whose summed `amount` over orders with order_date in the inclusive window [window_start, ref_date] = [2026-02-28, 2026-05-28] is strictly > 100. In-window totals: C001 = 80 + 60 = 140 (MATCH), C002 = 40 (<=100, NON-MATCH), C003 = 150 (MATCH; its single order is dated EXACTLY window_start 2026-02-28 -> boundary classified IN), C004 = 0 in-window (its only order 2026-02-27 is < window_start -> just OUTSIDE, NON-MATCH), C005 = 200 (MATCH), C006 = 0 (no orders, NON-MATCH). Matches: C001, C003, C005. Non-matches: C002, C004, C006. Boundary customer at window_start: C003 (IN). Just-outside customer: C004 (OUT). Plan 03 asserts exactly ['C001','C003','C005'].
